3/17/2026
hope this is not too much to read I just copied it :  About the job We’re looking for one Mid-Level Full-Stack Mobile Developer to join us for a focused 5-day paid product sprint. This is a short, high-intensity build sprint with an agile execution team. We are looking for developers who can move fast, collaborate tightly, and deliver production-ready work. You’ll collaborate closely with: Another Full-Stack  Frontend Developer A Product Owner A Business Executive About eFama eFama is a digital marketplace connecting farmers directly with buyers across Africa. Our platform supports the full value chain onboarding, verification, ordering, logistics, and payment helping farmers access markets while giving buyers reliable supply and pricing transparency. During the sprint you will: Refresh UX/UI across key mobile app flows Implement selected mobile features Improve usability and navigation Tighten design consistency Clean up and optimise parts of the codebase Prepare the app for stable publishing across iOS and Android Ship production-ready improvements within the sprint Improve performance and stability Integrate APIs and services Work within the existing codebase (no rewrites) We will be using AI-assisted development tools to accelerate delivery, so you should be comfortable working in a modern AI-enabled build environment. Requirements Mid-level (approx. 3–6 years experience) Experience working on live mobile apps Experience publishing apps to Apple App Store and Google Play Comfortable in fast-moving sprint environments Strong collaboration and problem-solving skills Available for the full 5-day sprint Able to work in our existing tech stack Strong JavaScript/TypeScript experience Mobile app development (React Native, Flutter or similar) Backend/API experience Git and modern dev workflows
Your personal messages are end-to-end encrypted

This morning I will be focusing on finalising the designs for the Bidvest beneficiary websites, ensuring that layouts, visual elements, and overall user experience are aligned with project expectations and ready for review. Once this is completed, I will proceed with resuming and completing the Anglo websites from last week, which were temporarily halted due to the Bidvest work, ensuring they are brought up to completion and ready for feedback or deployment. In addition, I will be collaborating with Thato on developing a new design direction for the ENS Foundation website, focusing on improving the overall look and feel, structure, and visual consistency of the platform. All tasks are structured to ensure continued progress across projects while maintaining quality and alignment with deliverables.22:11
